title: STANDARD FOR AUBERGINES CXS 330-2018 Adopted in 2018.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR AUBERGINES CXS 330-2018 Adopted in 2018.
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Aubergines (CXS 330‑2018), adopted in 2018 (document heading).

Defines the Standard's scope as quality requirements for commercial aubergines (Solanum melongena L.) after preparation and packaging for fresh supply, notes that slight loss of freshness/turgidity or slight deterioration post‑packaging may occur, and obliges the holder/seller to ensure products are marketed only in conformity with the Standard.

Defines the produce covered as commercial varieties of aubergine/eggplant (Solanum melongena L.) supplied fresh after preparation and packaging (excluding industrial processing) and classifies shapes as elongated, globus/round, or oval.
Introduces provisions concerning quality for aubergines, including minimum requirements and a three‑class classification system with specific quality criteria and allowances per class.

Sets minimum quality requirements for all classes: aubergines must be intact (calyx/peduncle may be slightly damaged), firm, fresh in appearance, sound (no rot), clean, practically free of pests and pest damage, free of significant bruising, abnormal external moisture, foreign smell/taste, or temperature damage, and sufficiently developed to withstand transport and arrive in satisfactory condition.
Requires aubergines to be sufficiently mature without fibrous/woody flesh or hard seeds, and notes that the pest provisions are without prejudice to plant protection rules under the IPPC.
States that aubergines are classified into three classes (detailed in subsequent sections).

Defines 'Extra' Class aubergines as superior quality, firm, characteristic of the variety/commercial type with intact stalk and sound flesh, and free of defects except slight superficial ones that do not affect appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

Defines Class I aubergines as good quality and characteristic of the variety/commercial type, allowing slight defects in shape, development, discoloration, superficial defects, slight bruising or healed cracks provided they do not affect general appearance, quality, keeping quality or presentation.

Defines Class II as aubergines that meet the minimum requirements but not higher classes, permitting defects in shape and development, variety‑dependent discoloration, slight bruising/healed cracks or sun‑scorch and slight dry skin defects provided the flesh and essential characteristics for quality, keeping and presentation are retained.

Allows sizing of aubergines by diameter, count, length, weight or trade practice (with the package labelled for method and size), prescribes maximum size range differences for uniformity (diameter: 20 mm for elongated, 25 mm for globus/oval) and weight‑class tolerances for specified weight bands, and makes size uniformity compulsory for Extra Class except for aubergines ≤30 mm diameter.

Allows tolerances by lot for produce not satisfying the indicated class and permits non‑conforming produce to be resorted and brought into conformity in accordance with the Guidelines for Food Import Control Systems (CXG 47‑2003).
Section heading for '5.1 Quality tolerances' (no substantive text beyond the heading).

Permits up to 5% by number or weight of Extra Class aubergines in a lot to fail Extra Class requirements provided they meet Class I, and allows a 1% tolerance within that 5% for decay, soft rot and/or internal breakdown.
For Class I aubergines, up to 10% by number or weight may consist of produce that meets Class II requirements, and within that allowance up to 1% may have decay, soft rot and/or internal breakdown.

For Class II aubergines, up to 10% by number or weight may fail to satisfy Class II and the minimum requirements, and within that allowance up to 2% may have decay, soft rot and/or internal breakdown.
If sized, for all classes up to 10% by number or weight of aubergines not meeting the sizing requirements is permitted.
Heading for provisions concerning presentation; no substantive requirements are given in this section.

Each package must be uniform in origin, variety/commercial type, quality, colour and size (if sized) with the visible contents representative of the whole, except that distinctly different commercial types may be mixed if each type is uniform in quality and origin.

Aubergines must be packed to protect the produce, using food-grade, clean materials that do not cause external or internal damage, printed materials only with non-toxic ink or glue, and packaging must comply with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).
Containers must have adequate quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance for handling, shipping and preserving aubergines and be free of foreign matter and smell.
Heading for provisions concerning marking and labelling; no substantive requirements are contained in this section.
For consumer packages, in addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 119-85), the specific labelling provisions in the following subsections apply.
Each consumer package must be labelled with the name of the produce and may include the name of the commercial type.

Packages must indicate the country of origin and may optionally show district or place name; for mixtures of distinctly different commercial types from different origins, the country of origin for each commercial type must appear next to its name.

Non-retail (bulk) containers must bear certain particulars grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked and visible from the outside; the details required are specified in subsequent subsections.
Non-retail containers must show the name and address of the exporter, packer and/or dispatcher, and may include an identification code.

Non-retail containers must state the name of the produce ('aubergines') and may give the commercial type; mixtures of distinct commercial types must be labelled as such and, if the produce is not visible, the commercial types and quantity of each must be indicated.

Non-retail containers must indicate the country of origin and may include district or place name, and for mixtures of different commercial types from different origins the country of origin for each variety must be shown next to its name.
Commercial specifications on non-retail containers must declare class and, if sized, size either as minimum and maximum equatorial diameters (mm) or by weight (g).
Indicates the official inspection mark is optional (no further detail provided in this subsection).
Aubergines must comply with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides and with the maximum levels set out in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

It is recommended that aubergines be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003) and related Codex texts, and they should comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 2-1997.
